rationale: Pre-commit Phase A test parameters BEFORE running any MC. Locks all SMA windows, weights, leverage, drift thresholds, vol windows, and data splices. Any change requires explicit re-commit with new version and rationale entry below.

shared.stop_phase_b_rule: if no Phase A primary hypothesis clears AND no test reaches mean log-excess > -0.05

data_splices.VTI_pre_2001_06_15: use SPY adjusted close (VTI tracks SPY very closely)
data_splices.IEF_pre_2002_07_30: synthetic via FRED DGS7 yield: daily TR ≈ -modified_duration(6.3) × Δyield_daily + (yield/252) accrual
data_splices.BND_pre_AGG: synthetic via FRED DGS5 yield: daily TR ≈ -modified_duration(5.5) × Δyield_daily + (yield/252) accrual
data_splices.GLD_pre_2004_11_18: use yfinance GC=F gold futures front-month price-change (no carry); note: futures-roll drag not modeled here, acceptable for pre-2004 splice given small fraction of total panel
data_splices.VGSH_pre_2009_11_23: use daily T-bill rate from FRED (3m T-bill / 252 daily accrual)
data_splices.BIL_pre_2007_05_30: use daily T-bill rate from FRED
data_splices.panel_start_constraint: 2000-08-30 = max of {GC=F inception, SPY inception, FRED tbill availability}; pre-2000-08 source data not used

test_1_sma200_monthly_overlay.strategy: 100% VTI when VTI close >= SMA200(VTI); else 100% VGSH
test_1_sma200_monthly_overlay.cadence_note: true calendar-month-end is impossible on bootstrap paths (no calendar). 21-trading-day cadence is the documented approximation. Validated trend_following config uses calendar monthly — this approximation is conservative (more frequent flips, slightly higher friction).
test_1_sma200_monthly_overlay.execution: T+1: signal computed at end of cadence period, applied to next trading day
test_1_sma200_monthly_overlay.primary_hypothesis: mean log-wealth excess vs VTI B&H >= 0
test_1_sma200_monthly_overlay.secondary_hypothesis: p5 max drawdown is less negative than VTI's p5 by >= 10 percentage points

test_2_vti_iau_static.strategy: 90% VTI / 10% IAU, annual rebalance, drift > 5pp triggers reset
test_2_vti_iau_static.primary_hypothesis: mean log-wealth excess vs VTI B&H >= 0
test_2_vti_iau_static.secondary_hypothesis: p5 max drawdown is less negative than VTI's p5 by >= 3 percentage points

test_4_vti_ief.strategy: 60% VTI / 40% IEF, annual rebalance, drift > 5pp triggers reset
test_4_vti_ief.secondary_hypothesis: p5 max drawdown vs 60/40 VTI/BND >= 0 (i.e., not worse)

test_7_tf_rp_blend.strategy: 40% trend-following + 60% inverse-vol risk parity (combined daily returns)

tf_component.rule: 100% VTI when above SMA200; else 100% VGSH

rp_component.weights_rule: inverse trailing 63d vol, normalized to sum=1; clipped to [0.05, 0.50] each

test_7_tf_rp_blend.secondary_hypothesis: p5 max drawdown is less negative than 60/40 VTI/BND's p5 by >= 5 percentage points

test_5_glidepath_lifecycle.rationale: 2x scaling of E2 3x params: borrow=25bps × (2-1)/(3-1)=12.5bps; SSO actual ER 0.89%

version_history.note: initial commit after adversarial review of plan

Post-Phase-A-v1 review: drift-trigger was specified in v1.0 but NOT implemented in run_phase_a.py (only annual_rebalance_returns called). v1.1 adds drift_aware_rebalance_returns to harness.py and uses it for Tests 2/4. Also adds placebo (gold-mean-shifted) and sub-period robustness blocks (2000-2010 / 2010-2026) per adversarial reviewer recommendations. Test 2 v1 finding (+0.134 log-excess vs VTI) suspected to be source-period carry artifact; v2 checks this directly.

Empirical check on 1000-path subsample: drift-trigger (5pp) vs annual-only produces +0.0004 log-excess difference for Test 2 (immaterial). 80x compute cost not justified. v1.2 uses annual_rebalance_returns for Tests 2/4 (and placebo), documents this honestly. drift_aware_rebalance_returns retained in harness.py for future strategies that may need it. drift_threshold field below remains for documentation but is not honored in the v1.2 implementation.
